reasoning_model_1: Model 1 successfully incorporates the required non-linguistic vocalization. The audio begins with a distinct coughing sound (00:00-00:01) before the speech begins. The spoken content 'I just choked, it is a bit uncomfortable' follows the script accurately (00:01-00:04). The prosody reflects a slight pause and breathiness consistent with someone recovering from choking, enhancing the naturalness. The non-linguistic feature is clearly audible and relevant to the prompt.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails to generate the required non-linguistic vocalization. Instead of producing the actual sound of coughing, the model hallucinates additional text, verbally describing the action ('and I'm coughing a little as I try to catch my breath') which was not part of the requested script. There is no actual coughing sound effect present in the audio. Consequently, it fails both the content accuracy criterion (by adding text) and the core paralinguistic criterion (by failing to produce the sound effect).
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately interprets the command 'with coughing' by generating the actual sound effect (non-linguistic vocalization) and adheres strictly to the provided text. Model 2 misunderstands the prompt, failing to produce the sound effect and instead appending unauthorized text describing the action. Model 2's score is 0 due to the complete absence of the target feature and the significant content error.
